title: senecio antofagastanus
intro: Senecio antofagastanus, also known as the Atacama groundsel, is a plant species in the daisy family native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Senecio antofagastanus is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with some studies suggesting potential antioxidant and antiviral activities.
